answer:
Worry level: high. Not "if" but "when." YouTube ships DOM changes continuously, A/B tests markup per-user, has history of breaking scrapers/extensions (adblock wars 2023-24 show they'll actively obfuscate). 80% acquisition on 1 fragile channel = existential single point of failure.

Realistic failure modes:
1. Selector breaks → extension silently dead for days before you notice.
2. YouTube A/B test → breaks for 10% of users, hard to debug.
3. Chrome Web Store policy action (MV3, scraping-adjacent behavior) → delisted, worse than breakage.

What to do, priority order:

**This week — detection & resilience:**
- Telemetry: heartbeat event when extension successfully finds DOM elements. Alert on drop >X%. Silent breakage is the real killer.
- Defensive selectors: multiple fallback strategies (data attrs, aria labels, text content, structural heuristics), not single CSS path.
- Remote config: fetch selector definitions from your server so you can patch w/o Chrome review cycle (2-7 days). This alone cuts downtime from days to hrs. Keep within CWS remote-code rules — config/data OK, executable code not.

**This month — reduce blast radius:**
- Automated canary: headless browser hitting YouTube hourly, running your selectors, alerting on failure. Catches breaks before users do.
- Check if YouTube Data API / InnerTube covers any of what you scrape — API-backed features survive DOM churn.
- On-call playbook: who fixes, target time-to-patch.

**This quarter — strategic:**
- Diversify acquisition: 80% from 1 channel you don't control = biggest business risk regardless of DOM. Push users to accounts/email immediately after install so a dead extension ≠ dead relationship.
- Model the kill scenario: if extension dies permanently tomorrow, what survives? Build toward that answer (web app, other platforms, SEO/content, partnerships).

Bottom line: DOM breakage is survivable w/ monitoring + remote config + fast patch loop (many extensions live this way for yrs). CWS delisting or deliberate YouTube countermeasures are not — hence diversify now while channel still works.
